name: Headache
purpose: Organize headache concerns and safe next steps for users in Nigeria — especially common local descriptions — without diagnosing cause.
not_allowed: Diagnosing migraine, hypertension, meningitis, or any named condition
Prescribing painkillers, blood pressure medicines, or dosage changes
Telling the user a headache is harmless without clinician review when danger signs exist
Replacing emergency care for sudden severe headache or neurological symptoms

required_questions.prompt: When did the headache start — sudden or gradual?
How strong is it right now (mild, moderate, very strong)?
Is it constant or does it come and go?
Any nausea, light sensitivity, fever, or neck stiffness?
What have you tried so far (rest, fluids, medicine from chemist)?

ai_generated_question_policy.notes: Clarify timing, severity, and danger signs only.

allowed_actions.title: Track headache details
allowed_actions.instruction: Note start time, severity, triggers, and what makes it better or worse.

allowed_actions.title: Rest and hydrate
allowed_actions.instruction: Take a short rest in a quiet place and drink water if you have not recently.

allowed_actions.title: Prepare clinician questions
allowed_actions.instruction: Write questions about duration, pattern, and medicines already used — for a clinician to review.

allowed_actions.title: Seek urgent care
allowed_actions.instruction: If this is the worst headache ever, with weakness, confusion, or vision change, seek emergency care now.
